Patch Set 6:

James Simmons, I'm in no position to inspect this patch on its technical merits, but since we are completely unable to test this ourselves, could you please include some comments (perhaps in the commit message) as to how this patch has been tested?

James Shimek, is this a patch that is being tested and/or running on current Cray systems that is just being landed to the upstream branch, or is this new development from ORNL?  Is this something that will go into your tree after it lands here?

Patch Set 6:

> James Shimek, is this a patch that is being tested and/or running on
> current Cray systems that is just being landed to the upstream branch,
> or is this new development from ORNL? Is this something that will go into
> your tree after it lands here?

Andreas, this is not running on production Cray systems yet and is James Simmons' development from ORNL.  We have started testing it though.  Yes, we will put it in our tree eventually.  We plan to pull and push regular (quarterly?) updates to keep us in sync.

Patch Set 10: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

James, could you please comment on what kind of testing this patch has had at ORNL? Ideally a description of the testing should fo into the commit comment. We are completely unable to test this ourselves.

I'm inclined to land this if it has some testing at ORNL, since there was no objection from Cray in an earlier version, and they will be doing their own build and test anyway.

Patch Set 10:

Could some LNET person do an inspection of the actual changes?

Patch Set 10: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(2 inline comments)

This should not be landed yet, the way schedulers threads are bound to cpt's and how connections have there own cpt can cause an issue where the scheduler thread does not wake up to send messages when kgnilnd_send is called. If forward progress is to be made the connections need to have the same CPT as the one the scheduler thread/s is/are bound to.

kgnilnd_queue_tx calls kgnilnd_schedule_conn which calls kgnilnd_schedule_device which takes the conns->gnc_sched.. Since this sched is might be a a different cpt than the ones that the actual scheduler threads are bound to nothing will happen and forward progress on the send will be stalled.

Looks good.  One general comment about the GNI LND: I noticed the coding style has braces around single statement "if" or "else" clauses.  I thought that is against the kernel coding guidelines and will need to be addressed at some point.

parents.subject: Revert "LU-8856 osd: mark specific transactions netfree"

commit.subject: LU-2544 lnet: SMP scaling api for Gemini driver
commit.message:
LU-2544 lnet: SMP scaling api for Gemini driver

Increase the performance of the Gemini LND driver by
using the Lustre SMP api. Bind the threads to specific
cores to limit the cost of thread migration between
different cores. The second improvement is allocating
memory based on which core so we can keep the cache
warm to limit cache line misses. Lastly we reduce the
amount of memory allocated for the cksum pages from
all online cpus to due the cpus actually used by the
LND driver.

Test-Parameters: trivial

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Change-Id: I0c1fca8d6ea21986cddfb615eb455ae09843a4dc
